occasion: Daily Prayer, Sandhya Vandanam, Upanayana
sanskrit: ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः । तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं । भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि । धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात् ॥
roman: Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah | Tat Savitur Varenyam | Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi | Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat ||
hindi: हम उस परमात्मा की भव्य शक्ति का ध्यान करते हैं जो तीनों लोकों को प्रकाशित करती है। वह हमारी बुद्धि को सन्मार्ग पर प्रेरित करे।
english: We meditate on the glorious light of the Divine Sun that illuminates the three worlds. May that light inspire and guide our intellect.

word_by_word.meaning: The primordial sound — the sacred syllable of Brahman
Bhu loka — the physical earth realm
That — referring to the Supreme Brahman
Of Savitri — the Divine Sun, source of all life
Most adorable, most worthy of worship
The radiant light, the destroyer of sins
May inspire, may guide, may illuminate

commentary_english: The Gayatri Mantra is the most sacred mantra of the Vedas, first appearing in the Rigveda (3.62.10). It is addressed to Savitri, the solar deity who represents the life-giving power of the sun. The three vyahritis (Bhur, Bhuvah, Svah) represent the three planes of existence — physical, astral, and celestial. Maharishi Vishwamitra is credited with receiving this mantra in deep meditation. Traditionally recited at dawn, noon, and dusk during Sandhya Vandanam, it is considered the mother of all mantras and is given to initiates during the Upanayana (sacred thread) ceremony. It is not merely a prayer for intellect — it is a meditation on the Supreme Light of Consciousness that underlies all existence.
commentary_hindi: गायत्री मंत्र वेदों का सबसे पवित्र मंत्र है, जो ऋग्वेद (3.62.10) में पहली बार प्रकट हुआ। यह सवितृ देवता को समर्पित है। तीन व्याहृतियां (भूर्, भुवः, स्वः) अस्तित्व के तीन स्तरों का प्रतिनिधित्व करती हैं। महर्षि विश्वामित्र ने इस मंत्र को गहन ध्यान में प्राप्त किया था। यह उपनयन संस्कार में दीक्षार्थियों को दिया जाता है और संध्यावंदन में प्रातः, मध्याह्न और सायं जपा जाता है।
audio_hint: Recite slowly with 3 natural pauses at each | mark. 108 repetitions (japa) at dawn is ideal.

occasion: Healing, Protection, Overcoming Death, Shiva Puja
sanskrit: ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम् । उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान् मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात् ॥
roman: Om Tryambakam Yajamahe Sugandhim Pushtivardhanam | Urvarukamiva Bandhanaan Mrityor Mukshiya Maamritat ||
hindi: हम तीन नेत्रों वाले भगवान शिव की पूजा करते हैं, जो सुगंधित हैं और सभी को पोषण देते हैं। जैसे ककड़ी अपनी लता से मुक्त हो जाती है, वैसे ही हमें मृत्यु के बंधन से मुक्त करें, अमरत्व की ओर ले जाएं।
english: We worship the three-eyed Lord Shiva who is fragrant and who nourishes all. Like a cucumber freed from its vine, may He liberate us from the bondage of death toward immortality.

word_by_word.meaning: The nourisher of all, the increaser of strength
From immortality, from the nectar of life

commentary_english: The Mahamrityunjaya Mantra — the Great Death-Conquering Mantra — is one of the most potent Vedic mantras. Composed by Maharishi Markandeya, it is addressed to Lord Shiva in his form as Tryambaka (the three-eyed one). The three eyes represent the sun, moon, and fire — or past, present, and future. The mantra uses the beautiful metaphor of a ripe cucumber naturally separating from its vine — death not as a violent tearing, but as a gentle, natural release into immortality. It is chanted during illness, surgeries, and times of danger, and is considered as powerful as the Gayatri Mantra itself.
commentary_hindi: महामृत्युञ्जय मंत्र — महान मृत्युविजयी मंत्र — सबसे शक्तिशाली वैदिक मंत्रों में से एक है। महर्षि मार्कण्डेय द्वारा रचित, यह भगवान शिव के त्र्यम्बक रूप को संबोधित है। तीन नेत्र सूर्य, चंद्र और अग्नि का प्रतीक हैं। मंत्र में पकी ककड़ी के स्वाभाविक रूप से बेल से अलग होने का सुंदर रूपक है। इसे बीमारी, शल्यक्रिया और संकट के समय जपा जाता है।
audio_hint: Chant 108 times with a rudraksha mala, especially on Mondays and Shiva Chaturdashi.

occasion: Opening Prayer, Study of Upanishads, Peace Ritual
sanskrit: ॐ पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदं पूर्णात्पूर्णमुदच्यते । पूर्णस्य पूर्णमादाय पूर्णमेवावशिष्यते ॥ ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः ।
roman: Om Purnamadah Purnamidam Purnat Purnamudachyate | Purnasya Purnamadaya Purnamevavashishyate || Om Shantih Shantih Shantih |
hindi: वह (परब्रह्म) पूर्ण है, यह (जगत) भी पूर्ण है। पूर्ण से ही पूर्ण की उत्पत्ति होती है। पूर्ण में से पूर्ण को निकाल लेने पर भी पूर्ण ही शेष रहता है। ॐ शांति शांति शांति।
english: That is whole, this is whole. From wholeness, wholeness arises. When wholeness is taken from wholeness, wholeness alone remains. Om Peace Peace Peace.

word_by_word.meaning: That — the unmanifest Brahman beyond perception
This — the manifest world of experience
From that wholeness, from the infinite
Peace — three times for peace in body, mind, and spirit

commentary_english: This Shanti Path (peace invocation) opens the Isha Upanishad, one of the shortest yet most profound of all Upanishads. It expresses the core Advaita (non-dual) philosophy: Brahman (the Absolute) is infinite and complete. The manifest universe that arose from it is also complete. Even subtracting infinity from infinity, infinity remains — a mathematical truth that mirrors spiritual reality. The triple repetition of Shantih invokes peace at three levels: the physical (adhibhautika), the divine/cosmic (adhidaivika), and the individual/mental (adhyatmika).
commentary_hindi: यह शांति पाठ ईशावास्योपनिषद् का उद्घाटन श्लोक है, जो सबसे छोटे किंतु सबसे गहन उपनिषदों में से एक है। यह अद्वैत दर्शन का सार है — ब्रह्म पूर्ण है, जगत भी पूर्ण है, और पूर्ण में से पूर्ण निकालने पर भी पूर्ण शेष रहता है। शांति का तीन बार उच्चारण तीन तापों — आधिभौतिक, आधिदैविक और आध्यात्मिक — से मुक्ति के लिए है।
audio_hint: Chant the three Shantih slowly and with full breath, allowing silence between each.

occasion: Daily Contemplation, Renunciation, Non-attachment
sanskrit: ईशावास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत् । तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथाः मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम् ॥
roman: Ishavaasyamidam Sarvam Yatkincha Jagatyam Jagat | Tena Tyaktena Bhunjithah Ma Gridhah Kasyasviddhanam ||
hindi: इस जगत में जो कुछ भी है — चर और अचर — सब ईश्वर से आच्छादित है। उस त्याग की भावना से उपभोग करो। किसी के धन का लोभ मत करो।
english: All this — whatever moves in this moving world — is pervaded by the Lord. Enjoy through renunciation; do not covet the wealth of anyone.

word_by_word.meaning: Pervaded by the Lord, inhabited by Isha
In the world, in this moving universe
Do not covet, do not be greedy
Of anyone, whose is it anyway?

commentary_english: The very first verse of the Isha Upanishad contains the essence of the entire Upanishadic teaching. Everything in this universe — animate and inanimate — is pervaded by the divine consciousness of Isha (the Lord). Because everything belongs to the Lord, the wise person enjoys the world through the spirit of renunciation — using things without clinging to them. This verse resolves the apparent conflict between worldly life and spirituality: you need not abandon the world; simply abandon the sense of ownership. It also anticipates Gita 2.47 — do your work without craving the fruits.
commentary_hindi: ईशावास्योपनिषद् का पहला श्लोक संपूर्ण उपनिषद् की शिक्षा का सार है। इस ब्रह्मांड में जो कुछ भी है — चर-अचर — सब ईश्वर से व्याप्त है। क्योंकि सब कुछ ईश्वर का है, इसलिए त्याग की भावना से उपभोग करो। यह श्लोक गीता के 2.47 की भावना का पूर्वाभास देता है।
audio_hint: Contemplate one phrase at a time. Sit with 'Ishavaasyam idam sarvam' before moving to the next.

occasion: Deep Meditation, Self-inquiry, Om Japa
sanskrit: ॐ इत्येतदक्षरमिदं सर्वं तस्योपव्याख्यानं भूतं भवद्भविष्यदिति सर्वमोंकार एव । यच्चान्यत्त्रिकालातीतं तदप्योंकार एव ॥
roman: Om Ityetadaksharam Idam Sarvam Tasyopavyakhyanam Bhutam Bhavadbhavishyaditi Sarvamomkara Eva | Yachchanyattrikaalateetam Tadapyomkara Eva ||
hindi: ओम् — यह अक्षर ही यह सब है। इसकी व्याख्या करें तो — जो भूत, भविष्य और वर्तमान है, वह सब ओंकार ही है। और जो त्रिकाल से परे है, वह भी ओंकार ही है।
english: Om — this syllable is all this. Its explanation: All that is past, present, and future — all of this is the syllable Om. And whatever transcends the three times — that too is Om alone.

word_by_word.meaning: The sacred primordial syllable — A + U + M
That which transcends all three times

commentary_english: The Mandukya Upanishad — the shortest at 12 verses — is considered by Adi Shankaracharya to be sufficient alone for liberation. This opening verse establishes Om as the absolute reality. The syllable Om contains three sounds — A, U, M — corresponding to the waking, dreaming, and deep sleep states of consciousness. The silence after Om represents the fourth state, Turiya — pure consciousness, Brahman. Everything in time and beyond time is Om. This Upanishad is the foundation of Advaita Vedanta.
commentary_hindi: माण्डूक्योपनिषद् — केवल 12 श्लोकों में — आदि शंकराचार्य के अनुसार अकेले मोक्ष के लिए पर्याप्त है। यह उद्घाटन श्लोक ओम को परम सत्य के रूप में स्थापित करता है। ओम में तीन ध्वनियां — अ, उ, म — जागृत, स्वप्न और सुषुप्ति अवस्थाओं के अनुरूप हैं। ओम के बाद का मौन तुरीय — शुद्ध चेतना — का प्रतीक है।
audio_hint: Chant Om slowly: A arises from the belly, U rises through the chest, M vibrates at the lips. Then rest in silence.

title_english: Kena Upanishad — Who Moves the Mind?
occasion: Self-inquiry, Philosophical Contemplation
sanskrit: केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः । केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति ॥
roman: Keneshitam Patati Preshitam Manah Kena Pranah Prathamah Praiti Yuktah | Keneshitam Vachamidam Vadanti Chakshu Shrotram Ka U Devo Yunakti ||
hindi: किसके द्वारा प्रेरित होकर मन अपने विषय की ओर जाता है? किसके निर्देश से प्राण अपना कार्य करता है? किससे प्रेरित होकर मनुष्य यह वाणी बोलता है? कौन देव नेत्र और कर्ण को नियुक्त करता है?
english: Willed by whom does the directed mind go toward its objects? Directed by whom does life, the first breath, move? By whose will do men utter speech? Which deity directs the eyes and ears?

word_by_word.meaning: Goes toward, falls upon, flies to
The vital breath, the life force

commentary_english: The Kena Upanishad — 'Kena' means 'by whom' — begins with the most fundamental question of self-inquiry. The student asks the teacher: Who or what power moves my mind, activates my breath, enables my speech, sight, and hearing? The answer, developed through the Upanishad, is Brahman — the universal consciousness that is the ground of all functions. Yet paradoxically, Brahman cannot itself be an object of the senses: 'That which is not thought by the mind, but by which the mind thinks — know that alone as Brahman.' This verse is the beginning of the inquiry 'Who am I?' that Ramana Maharshi later revived.
commentary_hindi: केनोपनिषद् — 'केन' का अर्थ है 'किसके द्वारा' — सबसे मूलभूत आत्म-जिज्ञासा से आरंभ होता है। छात्र पूछता है: कौन मेरे मन को चलाता है, प्राण को सक्रिय करता है, वाणी, दृष्टि और श्रवण को संभव बनाता है? उत्तर है ब्रह्म। रमण महर्षि ने बाद में इसी जिज्ञासा को 'मैं कौन हूं?' के रूप में पुनर्जीवित किया।
audio_hint: Sit in stillness and ask each question slowly. Don't seek an immediate answer — let the question open you.

occasion: Morning Prayer, Study Beginning, Universal Prayer
sanskrit: असतो मा सद्गमय । तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय । मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय । ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः ॥
roman: Asato Ma Sadgamaya | Tamaso Ma Jyotirgamaya | Mrityorma Amritam Gamaya | Om Shantih Shantih Shantih ||
hindi: मुझे असत्य से सत्य की ओर ले चलो। मुझे अंधकार से प्रकाश की ओर ले चलो। मुझे मृत्यु से अमृत की ओर ले चलो। ॐ शांति शांति शांति।
english: Lead me from the unreal to the Real. Lead me from darkness to Light. Lead me from death to Immortality. Om Peace Peace Peace.

word_by_word.meaning: From the unreal, from falsehood, from ignorance
To the Real, to Truth, to Brahman
Lead me, take me, guide me
To light, to knowledge, to enlightenment

commentary_english: This ancient prayer from the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad is perhaps the most universally beloved of all Vedic prayers. Its three paired movements — unreal/Real, darkness/Light, death/Immortality — map perfectly onto the Vedantic understanding of ignorance and liberation. 'Unreal' (asat) refers to the phenomenal world of appearances; 'Real' (sat) is Brahman, the eternal. 'Darkness' is the ignorance that makes us identify with the body-mind; 'Light' is self-knowledge. 'Death' is the limited ego-self; 'Immortality' is the recognition of one's true nature as deathless Atman. Each line is a complete spiritual path.
commentary_hindi: बृहदारण्यकोपनिषद् की यह प्रार्थना संभवतः सभी वैदिक प्रार्थनाओं में सबसे सार्वभौमिक रूप से प्रिय है। इसके तीन जोड़े — असत्/सत्, तमस/ज्योति, मृत्यु/अमृत — वेदांत के अनुसार अज्ञान से मुक्ति की यात्रा का सुंदर मानचित्र हैं। 'असत्' माया का जगत है, 'सत्' ब्रह्म है। प्रत्येक पंक्ति एक पूर्ण आध्यात्मिक पथ है।
audio_hint: Chant each of the three lines as a separate meditation. Pause between each and feel the movement within.

occasion: Vishnu Puja, Ekadashi, Satyanarayan Puja
sanskrit: शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम् । लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम् ॥
roman: Shaantaakaaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanaabham Suresham Vishvaadhaaaram Gaganasadrisham Meghavaranam Shubhangam | Lakshmeekaantam Kamalanayanam Yogibhirdhyaanagamyam Vande Vishnum Bhavabhayaharam Sarvalokaikanatham ||
hindi: जिनकी आकृति शांत है, जो शेषनाग पर शयन करते हैं, जिनकी नाभि से कमल प्रकट हुआ, जो देवों के ईश्वर हैं, जो विश्व के आधार हैं, जो आकाश के समान हैं, जो मेघ के समान वर्ण के हैं, जो शुभ अंगों वाले हैं, जो लक्ष्मी के प्रिय हैं, जो कमल-नयन हैं, जो योगियों के ध्यान से प्राप्त होते हैं — मैं उन विष्णु को प्रणाम करता हूं जो संसार के भय को हरते हैं और सब लोकों के एकमात्र स्वामी हैं।
english: I bow to Vishnu — whose form is serene, who reclines on the serpent Shesha, from whose navel the lotus arose, who is Lord of the gods, who supports the universe, who is like the sky, whose complexion is like a cloud, whose body is auspicious, who is the beloved of Lakshmi, whose eyes are like lotuses, who is attained by yogis through meditation, who removes the fear of worldly existence, and who is the sole Lord of all worlds.

word_by_word.meaning: From whose navel the lotus arose
Remover of the fear of worldly existence

commentary_english: This dhyana (meditation) shloka opens the Vishnu Sahasranama — the Thousand Names of Vishnu from the Mahabharata's Anushasana Parva. Each line paints a visual aspect of Vishnu for the meditator to hold in mind before chanting the thousand names. The form described — reclining on Shesha in the cosmic ocean, peaceful, blue like a monsoon cloud, lotus-eyed — is the iconic form of Anantashayana Vishnu. This visualization practice (dharana) is the traditional gateway into the depth of the Sahasranama's meaning.
commentary_hindi: यह ध्यान श्लोक विष्णु सहस्रनाम का उद्घाटन है — महाभारत के अनुशासन पर्व से विष्णु के एक हजार नाम। प्रत्येक पंक्ति ध्यानकर्ता के लिए विष्णु के रूप का एक दृश्य पहलू चित्रित करती है। शेषनाग पर शांत भाव से लेटा हुआ, नीले मेघ के समान वर्ण, कमल-नयन — यह अनंतशायी विष्णु का प्रतिष्ठित रूप है।
audio_hint: Close your eyes and visualize each image as you chant each phrase. Use this as a preparation before the full Sahasranama.

title_english: Ya Devi Sarva Bhuteshu — Durga Saptashati
occasion: Navratri, Durga Puja, Devi Worship
sanskrit: या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता । नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः ॥
roman: Ya Devi Sarva Bhuteshu Shakti Rupena Samsthita | Namastasyai Namastasyai Namastasyai Namo Namah ||
hindi: जो देवी सभी प्राणियों में शक्ति के रूप में विराजमान हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार, नमस्कार, नमस्कार।
english: Salutations to the Goddess who abides in all beings in the form of Shakti (energy/power). Salutations to Her, Salutations to Her, Salutations to Her again and again.

word_by_word.meaning: That Goddess, She who is divine
In all beings, in all creatures
In the form of Shakti, as energy/power
She who is established, who abides
Salutations to Her (repeated three times for emphasis)
Again and again I bow, infinite salutations

commentary_english: This verse from the Durga Saptashati (also called Devi Mahatmya, embedded in the Markandeya Purana) is repeated multiple times, each time substituting a different quality of the Goddess: Shakti (power), Buddhi (intellect), Nidra (sleep), Hunger, Consciousness, Compassion, Beauty, Memory, Illusion... The verse teaches that every force of nature and every quality in every being is a manifestation of the Divine Feminine. This is the heart of Shakta philosophy: the Goddess is not distant — She is the very energy animating you, the wisdom in your mind, the love in your heart. The triple Namah is an act of complete surrender.
commentary_hindi: दुर्गा सप्तशती का यह श्लोक कई बार दोहराया जाता है, हर बार देवी की अलग शक्ति का उल्लेख करते हुए: शक्ति, बुद्धि, निद्रा, क्षुधा, चेतना, करुणा... यह श्लोक सिखाता है कि प्रकृति की हर शक्ति और हर प्राणी में हर गुण दिव्य माँ का प्रकटीकरण है। शाक्त दर्शन का हृदय यही है। त्रिगुणात्मक नमः पूर्ण समर्पण का भाव है।
audio_hint: Chant 9 times during Navratri, substituting a different manifestation each time. The repetition itself is the teaching.

occasion: Before Starting Any Work, Time of Failure, Daily Contemplation
sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन । मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
roman: Karmanyevadhikaraste Ma Phaleshu Kadachana | Ma Karmaphalahetur Bhur Ma Te Sango Stvakarmani ||
hindi: तुम्हारा अधिकार केवल कर्म करने में है, उसके फलों में कभी नहीं। फल की कामना से कर्म मत करो, और कर्म न करने में भी आसक्त मत हो।
english: You have the right to perform your actions, but never to the fruits of your actions. Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let there be attachment to inaction.

word_by_word.meaning: In the fruits, in the results
One whose motive is the fruit of action

commentary_english: Gita 2.47 is possibly the single most quoted verse from the Bhagavad Gita and the cornerstone of Karma Yoga. Sri Krishna teaches Arjuna — and through him, all humanity — the secret of action without anxiety. Three teachings are packed into one verse: First, your right is to act, not to control outcomes. Second, don't make desire for fruit your motive — this poisons every action with anxiety. Third, don't retreat into inaction as an escape. The verse does not mean indifference to results; it means complete presence and excellence in the action itself, unclouded by the desperate need for a specific outcome.
commentary_hindi: गीता 2.47 संभवतः संपूर्ण भगवद्गीता का सर्वाधिक उद्धृत श्लोक है और कर्मयोग की आधारशिला है। तीन शिक्षाएं एक श्लोक में: प्रथम, तुम्हारा अधिकार कर्म है, परिणाम पर नियंत्रण नहीं। द्वितीय, फल की इच्छा से कर्म मत करो — यह प्रत्येक कार्य को चिंता से विषाक्त कर देता है। तृतीय, बचने के लिए अकर्म में मत भागो। इसका अर्थ परिणामों के प्रति उदासीनता नहीं है — इसका अर्थ है कर्म में पूर्ण उपस्थिति।
audio_hint: Memorize this verse. Recite it before any important task, exam, or meeting — as a reminder to give your best and release the rest.

title_english: Na Jayate Mriyate — The Eternal Soul
title_hindi: न जायते म्रियते — आत्मा की अनश्वरता
occasion: Grief, Death of a Loved One, Fear of Death
sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः । अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
roman: Na Jayate Mriyate Va Kadachin Nayam Bhutva Bhavita Va Na Bhuyah | Ajo Nityah Shashvato Ayam Purano Na Hanyate Hanyamane Sharire ||
hindi: यह आत्मा न कभी जन्म लेती है और न मरती है। यह पहले होकर फिर नहीं होती ऐसा भी नहीं है। यह अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत और पुरातन है। शरीर के मारे जाने पर भी यह नहीं मारी जाती।
english: The soul is never born nor dies at any time. It has not come into being and will not come into being again. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.

word_by_word.meaning: Is never born, does not take birth
Is not killed, is not slain
When the body is being slain

commentary_english: This verse from the Sankhya Yoga chapter (Ch. 2) is the heart of Krishna's teaching on the nature of the Atman. Delivered to Arjuna who is paralyzed with grief at the thought of killing his relatives, Krishna reveals that what appears to be death is only the shedding of a body. The Atman — the true Self — is beyond time, beyond causation, beyond all change. Four qualities are given: unborn (Aja), eternal (Nitya), ever-existing (Shashvata), and primeval (Purana). These four qualities are not merely philosophical claims — they are to be realized directly through meditation.
commentary_hindi: यह श्लोक सांख्य योग अध्याय (2) में है और आत्मा के स्वरूप पर कृष्ण की शिक्षा का हृदय है। अपने प्रियजनों को मारने की सोच से शोकाकुल अर्जुन को कृष्ण बताते हैं कि जो मृत्यु दिखती है, वह केवल शरीर का बदलाव है। आत्मा को चार गुणों से वर्णित किया गया है: अज (अजन्मा), नित्य, शाश्वत और पुराण।
audio_hint: Recite this when facing grief over the loss of a loved one. Let each quality — unborn, eternal, ever-existing, primeval — sink in deeply.

occasion: Bhakti Practice, Surrender, Times of Need
sanskrit: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते । तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
roman: Ananyashchintayanto Mam Ye Janah Paryupasate | Tesham Nityabhiyuktanam Yogakshemam Vahamyaham ||
hindi: जो अनन्य भाव से मेरा चिंतन करते हुए मेरी उपासना करते हैं, उन नित्य योगियों का योगक्षेम (जो नहीं है उसकी प्राप्ति और जो है उसकी रक्षा) मैं स्वयं वहन करता हूं।
english: For those who worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.

word_by_word.meaning: With no other, with exclusive focus
Who worship, who serve with devotion
Of those who are ever-steadfast, always united
Yoga (acquiring what one does not have) + Kshema (protecting what one has)

commentary_english: Gita 9.22 is the divine promise of Bhakti Yoga. Krishna does not say 'I will help' or 'I will guide' — He says 'I personally carry.' The Sanskrit word 'vahami' (I carry) is the word used for a porter carrying a load on his shoulder. This is the extraordinary intimacy of the Bhakti relationship. 'Yoga' here means acquiring what you lack; 'Kshema' means protecting what you already have. The verse is so beloved in Andhra Pradesh that it is inscribed on the entrance of Tirupati Balaji temple. The condition is 'ananya' — not distracted worship, but single-pointed, exclusive love.
commentary_hindi: गीता 9.22 भक्ति योग का दिव्य वचन है। कृष्ण 'सहायता करूंगा' नहीं, 'स्वयं वहन करूंगा' कहते हैं। 'वहामि' शब्द उस कुली का है जो बोझ अपने कंधे पर उठाता है। 'योग' का अर्थ है जो नहीं है उसकी प्राप्ति, 'क्षेम' का अर्थ है जो है उसकी रक्षा। शर्त है 'अनन्य' — अविभाजित, एकमात्र प्रेम।
audio_hint: Recite when you feel overwhelmed or lacking. Remember: the carrier is not you — let Krishna carry.

occasion: Total Surrender, Time of Confusion, Deepest Bhakti
sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान् परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज । अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
roman: Sarva Dharman Parityajya Mamekam Sharanam Vraja | Aham Tvam Sarvapapebhyo Mokshayishyami Ma Shuchah ||
hindi: सब धर्मों को त्यागकर एकमात्र मेरी शरण में आओ। मैं तुम्हें सभी पापों से मुक्त कर दूंगा, शोक मत करो।
english: Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.

word_by_word.meaning: All duties, all dharmas, all prescribed actions
Having abandoned, having completely given up
I — the personal promise of God
From all sins, from all transgressions
I shall liberate, I shall free
Do not grieve, do not be sorrowful

commentary_english: This is the Charama Shloka — the final, supreme verse — the last direct instruction of the Bhagavad Gita before Krishna asks Arjuna if he has truly listened. After 700 verses of philosophy, yoga, devotion, and knowledge, Krishna distills everything into two lines. 'Abandon all dharmas' does not mean abandon morality — it means abandon the burden of calculating which duty is highest. Stop the mental calculus of 'am I doing enough?' and simply surrender. The promise 'Aham tvam... mokshayishyami' — I personally will liberate you — is the most direct divine promise in all scripture. The final words 'ma shuchah' (do not grieve) mirror the very first words spoken to Arjuna in Chapter 2.
commentary_hindi: यह 'चरम श्लोक' है — भगवद्गीता का अंतिम, सर्वोच्च श्लोक, कृष्ण का आखिरी प्रत्यक्ष निर्देश। 700 श्लोकों के दर्शन, योग और भक्ति के बाद, कृष्ण सब कुछ दो पंक्तियों में समेट देते हैं। 'सर्वधर्मान् परित्यज्य' का अर्थ नैतिकता का त्याग नहीं है — इसका अर्थ है 'मैं पर्याप्त कर रहा हूं या नहीं?' की मानसिक गणना का बोझ छोड़ना और बस शरण लेना।
audio_hint: This verse is for your darkest moment. When you don't know what to do — recite this and simply surrender.

title_english: Yada Yada Hi Dharmasya — Avatara Declaration
title_hindi: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य — अवतार घोषणा
occasion: Understanding Providence, Faith During Dark Times
sanskrit: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत । अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
roman: Yada Yada Hi Dharmasya Glanirbhavati Bharata | Abhyutthanamadharmasya Tadatmanam Srijamyaham ||
hindi: हे भारत! जब-जब धर्म की हानि और अधर्म की वृद्धि होती है, तब-तब मैं अपने आप को प्रकट करता हूं।
english: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in righteousness, O descendant of Bharata, and a rise of unrighteousness — at that time I manifest Myself.

word_by_word.meaning: Whenever and wherever, at whatever time
I manifest, I create, I project

commentary_english: This verse introduces the doctrine of avatara — the divine descent. Krishna tells Arjuna that whenever dharma (cosmic order, righteousness, truth) declines and adharma rises, He personally manifests. This is not limited to past avatars — it is a timeless principle operating in every age, every civilization. Each avatar comes with the precise tools needed for that particular imbalance: Rama came when trickery and ego violated cosmic order; Krishna came when political corruption and moral confusion paralyzed society. This verse is also deeply personal — the Lord descends not just in history but in the life of every sincere seeker at their moment of crisis.
commentary_hindi: यह श्लोक अवतार सिद्धांत का परिचय देता है। कृष्ण बताते हैं कि जब-जब धर्म का ह्रास और अधर्म का उत्थान होता है, वे स्वयं प्रकट होते हैं। यह केवल ऐतिहासिक अवतारों तक सीमित नहीं है — यह हर युग में सक्रिय कालातीत नियम है। यह श्लोक व्यक्तिगत स्तर पर भी सत्य है — ईश्वर हर सच्चे साधक के जीवन में संकट के क्षण में अवतरित होते हैं।
audio_hint: Recite this when the world feels overwhelmed by darkness. It is a promise that balance will be restored.

occasion: Universal Prayer, Closing Any Puja, Beginning of Study
sanskrit: सर्वेषाम् स्वस्तिर्भवतु । सर्वेषाम् शान्तिर्भवतु । सर्वेषाम् पूर्णम्भवतु । सर्वेषाम् मङ्गलम्भवतु ॥ सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः । सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु मा कश्चिद्दुःखभाग्भवेत् ॥
roman: Sarvesham Svastir Bhavatu | Sarvesham Shantir Bhavatu | Sarvesham Purnam Bhavatu | Sarvesham Mangalam Bhavatu || Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah Sarve Santu Niramayah | Sarve Bhadrani Pashyantu Ma Kashchid Duhkha Bhag Bhavet ||
hindi: सभी का कल्याण हो। सभी को शांति मिले। सभी पूर्ण हों। सभी का मंगल हो। सभी सुखी हों, सभी रोगमुक्त हों। सभी शुभ देखें, कोई भी दुख का भागी न हो।
english: May all be auspicious. May all have peace. May all be whole. May all be blessed. May all be happy. May all be free from illness. May all see what is auspicious. May none suffer.

word_by_word.meaning: One who is destined for suffering

commentary_english: This mantra is the fullest expression of the Vedic vision of universal welfare — sarva loka hitam (the good of all worlds). Unlike most mantras that seek personal benefit, this prayer asks for nothing for oneself — only for all beings to be happy, peaceful, healthy, and free from suffering. It is chanted at the end of prayers, pujas, and study sessions to dedicate all the merit accumulated to the welfare of all. The second part 'Sarve bhavantu sukhinah...' is also the famous Shanti Mantra from the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad tradition. Mahatma Gandhi reportedly recited this daily.
commentary_hindi: यह मंत्र वैदिक दृष्टि की सबसे पूर्ण अभिव्यक्ति है — सर्व लोक हितम्। अधिकांश मंत्रों के विपरीत जो व्यक्तिगत लाभ मांगते हैं, यह प्रार्थना स्वयं के लिए कुछ नहीं मांगती — केवल सभी प्राणियों के सुख, शांति, स्वास्थ्य और दुख से मुक्ति के लिए। महात्मा गांधी इसे प्रतिदिन पढ़ते थे।
audio_hint: Chant at the end of any prayer or study session, visualizing all beings — friend and stranger alike — in peace and happiness.

title_english: Tvameva Mata — Universal Prayer to God
occasion: Daily Prayer, Bedtime, Gratitude
sanskrit: त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव । त्वमेव विद्या द्रविणं त्वमेव त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवदेव ॥
roman: Tvameva Mata Cha Pita Tvameva Tvameva Bandhushcha Sakha Tvameva | Tvameva Vidya Dravinam Tvameva Tvameva Sarvam Mama Devadeva ||
hindi: तू ही मेरी माता है और तू ही पिता है। तू ही बंधु है और तू ही मित्र है। तू ही विद्या है और धन भी तू ही है। हे देवों के देव! मेरा सब कुछ तू ही है।
english: You alone are my mother and father. You alone are my kinsman and friend. You alone are my knowledge and wealth. O God of gods, You are everything to me.

word_by_word.meaning: God of gods, Lord of lords

commentary_english: This beloved prayer — one of the most widely chanted in Indian homes — addresses the divine in all possible roles of love and support. By saying 'You alone' (tvameva) for each relationship, the devotee simultaneously honors all human relationships as divine and transcends them — recognizing that the deepest need behind every relationship is actually the need for the Divine itself. It is a prayer of ultimate simplicity and ultimate depth. Often attributed to Pandit Shardha Ram Phillauri but its roots are ancient. Swami Vivekananda cited it as a model of total surrender.
commentary_hindi: यह प्रिय प्रार्थना — भारतीय घरों में सबसे अधिक गाई जाने वाली प्रार्थनाओं में से एक — प्रेम और समर्थन की सभी संभावित भूमिकाओं में ईश्वर को संबोधित करती है। प्रत्येक संबंध के लिए 'त्वमेव' (केवल आप) कहकर, भक्त एक साथ सभी मानवीय संबंधों को दिव्य सम्मान देता है और उन्हें पार करता है। स्वामी विवेकानंद ने इसे पूर्ण शरणागति के प्रतिमान के रूप में उद्धृत किया।
audio_hint: Recite slowly before sleep, feeling each word: Mother, Father, Friend, Knowledge, Wealth — all are You.

occasion: Beginning Any Work, Exam, Journey, Puja Opening
sanskrit: वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभ । निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा ॥
roman: Vakratunda Mahakaya Suryakoti Samaprabha | Nirvighnam Kuru Me Deva Sarva Karyeshu Sarvada ||
hindi: हे वक्रतुण्ड (टेढ़ी सूंड वाले), हे महाकाय (विशाल शरीर वाले), हे करोड़ों सूर्यों के समान तेजस्वी — हे देव, मेरे सभी कार्यों में सदा विघ्नों को दूर करें।
english: O Lord Ganesha, of curved trunk and mighty form, whose brilliance is like ten million suns — O Lord, please remove all obstacles from all my endeavors, always.

word_by_word.meaning: Of equal radiance, as brilliant as
In all works, in all endeavors

commentary_english: This is the most universally used invocation shloka — recited before beginning any auspicious work, any scripture study, any journey, any puja. Ganesha is the first deity worshipped in the Hindu tradition because He is the lord of beginnings and the remover of obstacles (Vighnaharta). The shloka addresses three aspects: His physical form (curved trunk, vast body), His radiance (brighter than millions of suns), and His grace (remove all obstacles). The request is not for success, but simply for the removal of obstacles — trusting that clear passage will lead to success through one's own effort.
commentary_hindi: यह सबसे सार्वभौमिक रूप से उपयोग किया जाने वाला आवाहन श्लोक है — किसी भी शुभ कार्य, शास्त्र-पाठ, यात्रा या पूजा से पहले पढ़ा जाता है। गणेश पहले देवता हैं क्योंकि वे आरंभ के स्वामी और विघ्नहर्ता हैं। श्लोक में तीन पहलू हैं: रूप, तेज और कृपा। प्रार्थना सफलता के लिए नहीं, केवल विघ्न-मुक्ति के लिए है।
audio_hint: Recite once clearly at the start of any new endeavor. One sincere recitation is worth more than a hundred distracted ones.

occasion: Before Study, Exams, Saraswati Puja, Vasant Panchami
sanskrit: या कुन्देन्दुतुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना । या ब्रह्माच्युतशंकरप्रभृतिभिर्देवैः सदा वन्दिता सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा ॥
roman: Ya Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala Ya Shubhra Vastra Avrita Ya Veena Vara Danda Mandita Kara Ya Shveta Padmasana | Ya Brahma Achyuta Shankara Prabhritibhir Devaih Sada Vandita Sa Mam Patu Sarasvati Bhagavati Nihshesha Jadya Apaha ||
hindi: जो कुन्द पुष्प, चंद्रमा, हिम और मोतियों की माला के समान श्वेत हैं, जो श्वेत वस्त्र पहनती हैं, जिनके हाथ वीणा के उत्तम दंड से सुशोभित हैं, जो श्वेत कमल पर विराजमान हैं, जो ब्रह्मा, विष्णु और शिव द्वारा सदा वंदित हैं — वे सरस्वती भगवती मुझे संपूर्ण जड़ता से मुक्त करें और मेरी रक्षा करें।
english: She who is white as the Kunda flower, the moon, frost, and a pearl necklace; who is clothed in white; whose hands are adorned with the finest veena; who is seated on a white lotus; who is always worshipped by Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva — may that Goddess Saraswati protect me and remove all dullness of the mind.

word_by_word.meaning: Kunda flower and moon — both snow-white
The excellent staff of the veena
Remover of all dullness, all inertia, all mental fog

commentary_english: This exquisite vandana (praise-poem) to Goddess Saraswati is a masterwork of Sanskrit poetry. Every image in the first two lines is brilliantly white — Kunda flower, moon, frost, pearl necklace, white garments, white lotus — creating a meditation on pure, luminous consciousness. Saraswati is the goddess of knowledge, speech, music, arts, and wisdom. Her veena represents the harmonics of the cosmos. Her white color is not the white of absence but the white of all light combined. The final plea 'nihshesha jadya apaha' — remove all inertia, all dullness — is the heart of the prayer: grant us the awakened, luminous intelligence that sees clearly.
commentary_hindi: सरस्वती की यह वन्दना संस्कृत काव्य की उत्कृष्ट कृति है। प्रथम दो पंक्तियों में हर छवि श्वेत है — कुंद, चंद्र, तुषार, मोती, श्वेत वस्त्र, श्वेत कमल। सरस्वती ज्ञान, वाणी, संगीत, कला और बुद्धि की देवी हैं। उनका वीणावादन ब्रह्मांड के सामंजस्य का प्रतीक है। अंतिम प्रार्थना 'निःशेषजाड्यापहा' — सभी जड़ता को दूर करो — इस वंदना का सार है।
audio_hint: Recite before studying, before writing, before any creative work. Visualize the brilliant white form as you chant.

occasion: Shivaratri, Monday Worship, Shiva Puja
source_ref: Adi Shankaracharya — Shiva Panchakshara Stotra, Verse 1
sanskrit: नागेन्द्रहाराय त्रिलोचनाय भस्माङ्गरागाय महेश्वराय । नित्याय शुद्धाय दिगम्बराय तस्मै नकाराय नमः शिवाय ॥
roman: Nagendra Haraya Trilochanaya Bhasma Anga Ragaya Maheshvaraya | Nityaya Shuddhaya Digambaraya Tasmai Nakaraya Namah Shivaya ||
hindi: जो नागराज को हार की भांति धारण करते हैं, जिनके तीन नेत्र हैं, जिनके अंगों पर भस्म लगी है, जो महेश्वर हैं, जो नित्य हैं, शुद्ध हैं, दिगम्बर हैं — उन 'न' अक्षर स्वरूप शिव को नमस्कार।
english: To Him who wears the king of serpents as a garland, who has three eyes, whose body is smeared with sacred ash, who is the great Lord, who is eternal, who is pure, who is clothed by the directions — to that Shiva, represented by the syllable 'Na', I bow.

word_by_word.meaning: To Him who wears the serpent-king as a garland
Three-eyed one — the third eye of wisdom
Whose body is smeared with sacred ash
Sky-clad — wearing the directions as garments
To the syllable 'Na' — first letter of Namah Shivaya

commentary_english: This is the first verse of the Shiva Panchakshara Stotra — the five-syllable hymn — composed by Adi Shankaracharya. Each of the five verses meditates on one syllable of 'Na-Ma-Shi-Va-Ya' (the Panchakshara mantra, ॐ नमः शिवाय). Shankaracharya, the greatest systematizer of Advaita Vedanta, was also one of its greatest devotional poets — a reminder that jnana and bhakti are not opposites. The imagery in this verse is classic Shiva iconography: serpent-garland, third eye, sacred ash, sky-clad — each element encoding a metaphysical teaching. The ash (bhasma) signifies the destruction of the ego; the serpent represents mastered kundalini; the sky-clad form means Shiva needs nothing — He is all.
commentary_hindi: यह ग्रंथ आदि शंकराचार्य द्वारा रचित पंचाक्षर स्तोत्र की पहली कड़ी है। पांच पदों में से प्रत्येक 'न-म-शि-व-य' के एक अक्षर का ध्यान करता है। शंकराचार्य — अद्वैत वेदांत के सबसे महान व्यवस्थापक — उतने ही महान भक्त कवि भी थे। भस्म अहंकार के नाश का, नाग सिद्ध कुंडलिनी का, दिगम्बरत्व अनासक्ति का प्रतीक है।
audio_hint: Chant on Mondays, Shivaratri, or at dawn. Begin with Om Namah Shivaya (the Panchakshara), then this stotra.

occasion: Before Upanishad Study, Peace Ritual, Wholeness Meditation
source_ref: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 5.1.1 / Isha Upanishad Shanti Path
sanskrit: ॐ पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदं पूर्णात्पूर्णमुदच्यते । पूर्णस्य पूर्णमादाय पूर्णमेवावशिष्यते ॥
roman: Om Purnamadah Purnamidam Purnat Purnamudachyate | Purnasya Purnamadaya Purnamevavashishyate ||
hindi: वह (परब्रह्म) पूर्ण है, यह (जगत) भी पूर्ण है। पूर्ण से पूर्ण की उत्पत्ति होती है। पूर्ण में से पूर्ण लेने पर भी पूर्ण ही शेष रहता है।
english: That (Brahman) is whole. This (creation) is whole. From wholeness, wholeness arises. When wholeness is taken from wholeness, wholeness alone remains.

word_by_word.meaning: Whole, complete, infinite — the core concept
This — the manifest world of name and form

commentary_english: This shloka — identical in text to the Isha Upanishad Shanti Path — appears here in its original home, the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad. The mathematical paradox it expresses is also a spiritual truth: Infinity minus Infinity equals Infinity. This shatters ordinary logic and points to the nature of Brahman — which is not subject to ordinary arithmetic. The universe did not reduce Brahman by coming into existence. Subtracting the infinite world from the infinite Brahman leaves Brahman unchanged. This is the answer to the question: how can Brahman be beyond all, and yet the world also be Brahman? Both are complete. Both are Brahman. The apparent duality is the teaching to be dissolved.
commentary_hindi: यह श्लोक ईशावास्योपनिषद् के शांतिपाठ के समान है किंतु इसका मूल घर बृहदारण्यकोपनिषद् है। जो गणितीय विरोधाभास यह व्यक्त करता है वह आध्यात्मिक सत्य भी है: अनंत में से अनंत घटाने पर भी अनंत ही शेष रहता है। ब्रह्मांड के प्रकट होने से ब्रह्म में कोई कमी नहीं आई। दोनों पूर्ण हैं। दोनों ब्रह्म हैं।
audio_hint: Sit in silence after chanting. Rest in the word 'Purnam' — let the idea of infinite wholeness dissolve all sense of lack.
